text:
At a local tavern sat a peculiar man with short, wispy hair and spectacles hanging by his thin lips. His name was Olly, and he'd been there since dawn. The landlord approached, curiosity twirling on his tongue.

"Ah, Olly! Heard tell of some mysterious occurrences?" he asked.

Olly nodded, a thin smile creasing his lips.

"That's not unusual. Every so often we get tales of ghosts or magic. Could be your case," the landlord mused, clinking the mug they shared together.

Olly sat quietly for a moment, gazing out at the flickering candlelight, before speaking. "I've lived here for years, best years of my life. A quiet life, no big adventures that draw the usual crowd."

Curiosity sparked anew.

"Except for the wind? It blows strange sometimes?" The landlord chuckled.

Olly squinted, "The way? No. It's gentle. But there are moments. A
